Problem: Some downstream systems expect only one file, so the export needs to produce a single file instead of multiple files. 

Parameter Used: 

  • SINGLE = TRUE 

What Happens: 

  • Snowflake writes all data into a single file 

Why Use This: 

  • Required when downstream systems expect a single file 
  • Best for small datasets (Note: Not recommended for large data as it reduces performance.)
  • Useful for testing or incremental loads 

 SCENARIO 5: Exporting large Snowflake data while controlling output file size. 

Problem: When unloading large volumes of data, you may want Snowflake to split the output into manageable file sizes instead of creating one very large file. 

Parameter Used: 

  • MAX_FILE_SIZE = <num> (in bytes) 

What Happens: 

  • Snowflake splits data into multiple files 
  • Each file tries to stay within the specified size 

Why files can be slightly LARGER: 

  • Snowflake does not split rows 
  • Formatting and metadata add extra size 

Why files can be SMALLER: 

  • Snowflake uses multiple workers (parallel processing) 
  • Data is split based on internal storage (micro-partitions) 

Important Understanding:

  • MAX_FILE_SIZE is a limit, not a strict rule 
  • Snowflake prioritizes performance over exact file size 

Example: If you set MAX_FILE_SIZE = 1,000,000 bytes (1 MB), you may still see files slightly larger than 1 MB and multiple smaller files due to parallel execution. 

You might see file names like Unload-File_0_6_3.csv. 

This means: 

  • 0 → Node ID 
  • 6 → Worker/Thread ID 
  • 3 → File sequence number 

This helps Snowflake manage files during parallel processing. 

Why Use This Parameter: 

  • Controls number of output files 
  • Balances performance vs file management 
  • Improves data transfer efficiency 

Final Thoughts 

Optional parameters in Snowflake are very powerful and help you optimize your integration flows based on your needs. 

Whether it is handling errors, managing storage, controlling file sizes, improving performance, these parameters give you better control over your data operations.

For more advanced and complete details on all available parameters, refer to the official Snowflake documentation for Bulk Upsert and Unload.

entry.title: Which Integration Scenario Are You In? 3 SAP BTP Guides to Help You Move Forward
entry.content:
As organizations accelerate their cloud transformation journeys, integration remains a critical foundation for connecting systems, processes, and data across landscapes.

Within the SAP BTP Value Map, we have published a series of knowledge-based articles to support you in different integration scenarios — whether you are building new capabilities, modernizing existing landscapes, or planning your migration to the cloud. SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps are included with every SAP cloud subscription. To access Value Map Groups, you need to login to the SAP Community using an S-user ID or a Universal ID.

Below are three key scenarios you might be facing - and how you can get started.

 

🔹Scenario 1: Building Integration Expertise on SAP BTP

If you are an integration consultant or enterprise architect looking to deepen your expertise in SAP’s integration strategy and technologies…

Or if you want to better understand:

  • Core integration capabilities
  • API Management and Event Integration
  • Integration architecture and best practices

👉You may find this guide highly valuable:
SAP Integration Suite Black Belt Empowerment Guide

This enablement program is designed to help you become a subject matter expert in SAP Integration Suite, providing structured learning content, best practices, and practical insights to support your integration journey.

 

🔹Scenario 2: Migrating from SAP Neo to Multi-Cloud (Cloud Foundry)

If you are currently running integration scenarios in the SAP BTP Neo environment and are considering your move to the multi-cloud foundation…

Or if you are asking:

  • What is the right path forward from Neo?
  • How do I migrate my existing integration content?
  • What changes should I expect in Cloud Foundry?

👉This guide walks you through the process step by step:
SAP Neo, Cloud Integration to SAP Integration Suite Migration Guide

It provides practical insights, screenshots, and structured guidance to help you transition your integration scenarios from Neo to SAP Integration Suite on Cloud Foundry.

 

🔹Scenario 3: Transitioning from SAP PI/PO to SAP Integration Suite

If your organization is planning to move away from SAP Process Integration / Process Orchestration (PI/PO)…

Or if you are exploring:

  • Why SAP Integration Suite is the strategic direction
  • How to approach the migration
  • What support services are available

👉You can refer to this article:
How to Migrate from SAP Process Orchestration to SAP Integration Suite through Support Services

This guide outlines the migration approach based on SAP Activate methodology, helping you understand key phases, available options, and how SAP services can support your transition.

 

🚀Start Your Integration Journey with Confidence

Whether you are building new integration capabilities or modernizing your existing landscape, the SAP BTP Value Map provides structured guidance, curated content, and expert insights to support you every step of the way.

👉Explore these articles in the SAP BTP Value Map and take the next step in your integration journey.

We look forward to your feedback and engagement in the community.

entry.title: Bridging the Physical-Digital Divide: Proof of Presence for SAP EAM, TM, and SuccessFactors
entry.content:
In our journey toward the Intelligent Enterprise, data integrity is everything. We rely on SAP as our single source of truth. However, when it comes to remote physical operations – whether a technician inspecting a pipeline in SAP EAM, a field crew logging hours in SuccessFactors, or a logistics provider reaching a warehouse dock in SAP EWM – verifying actual physical presence at a specific time remains a conundrum.

While standard GPS or static barcodes offer basic tracking, they can be manipulated and often lack the mathematical certainty required for rigorous compliance, payroll accuracy, and safety audits. To achieve true "4D compliance" (verifying location and time simultaneously), organizations need an offline-capable solution that seamlessly integrates into the SAP ecosystem.

That is why I developed IAT4D (Identity Attendance Tracking) platform - see a whitepaper. Combined with SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), it provides the foundation to integrate and automate these secure workflows across an enterprise. Check the iFlow code in my GitHub.

The Architecture: Secure, Offline, and Clean Core Compliant

IAT4D utilizes edge-compute identity chips (iMarks) embedded in durable materials for site tagging. These passive markers require no power source or satellite signals, operating entirely on standard Apple CoreNFC and ISO 7816 smart-card technology.

Here is how it integrates with our existing SAP environment to guarantee unspoofable proof of presence:

  1. The Interaction: A field worker uses a standard SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) application to tap their mobile device against the physical iMark at the site.
  2. Dynamic Signature: Instead of broadcasting a static ID, the iMark electronically signs a dynamic, one-time passcode using Ed25519 cryptography. This creates a unique tamper-resistant digital bundle containing the location and the time.
  3. SAP Integration Suite: The mobile app forwards this as a lightweight JSON payload to SAP Cloud Integration (CPI).
  4. Verification: Using a pre-built Groovy script, SAP CPI verifies the cryptographic signature against an individual verification key. If successful, the system of record updates instantly:

This approach aligns perfectly with SAP’s Clean Core strategy. By keeping the cryptographic verification within SAP BTP, we ensure that only clean, mathematically verified data feeds into S/4HANA or SuccessFactors.

Bringing cryptographic proof of presence into standard SAP workflows unlocks immediate operational excellence across several core modules:

SAP EAM / Plant Maintenance (PM) & FSM
For critical assets like remote HVAC systems, lifts, rail switches, or pipelines, compliance is non-negotiable. By affixing an iMark directly to the physical asset, a technician’s tap provides verifiable proof that a safety inspection or maintenance routine actually occurred on-site. This transforms routine maintenance logs into immutable, audit-ready compliance records.

SAP SuccessFactors & SAP Fieldglass
Tracking time and attendance for sparse field workers, remote construction crews, or third-party contractors often happens in environments where networked biometric scanners are impossible to deploy. A rugged, offline iMark across service area allows workers to securely check in using their mobile devices. This ensures absolute payroll accuracy and protects the integrity of HR data.

SAP TM / EWM / Global Track and Trace (GTT)
Supply chain visibility relies on accurate milestone tracking. Tapping an iMark at a loading dock, specific drop-off checkpoint, or secure warehouse zone guarantees that logistics providers have reached the precise physical location. This eliminates the unreliability of fluctuating GPS signals and dramatically strengthens the track and trace capabilities.

How is your organization currently managing attendance verification for remote assets and field teams? Let’s discuss in the comments below...

entry.title: MuleSoft vs SAP Integration Suite: Which One Fits Your Enterprise?
entry.content:
STRATEGY PAPER - INTEGRATION PLATFORM ASSESSMENT

SAP Integration Suite Vs MuleSoft Anypoint Platform

Choosing an integration platform is one of the most consequential infrastructure decisions an enterprise can make. Both SAP Integration Suite and MuleSoft Anypoint Platform are mature, enterprise-grade solutions. This paper offers an objective, fact-based view of where SAP Integration Suite delivers a distinct strategic advantage for organisations whose landscape is built on SAP.

 

1. Setting the Context

While MuleSoft Anypoint Platform has long maintained a strong presence in the enterprise integration space, SAP Integration Suite, a core component of the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) has gained significant traction among organizations pursuing cloud-first and SAP-centric strategies.

Many enterprises are reassessing their reliance on MuleSoft as part of broader integration modernization initiatives. Factors such as rising total cost of ownership, challenges in optimizing integrations within SAP-heavy landscapes, and increasing demand for faster, scalable innovation are contributing to this shift.

As a result, SAP Integration Suite is increasingly being considered as a strategic alternative for organizations seeking tighter SAP alignment, improved cost efficiency, and enhanced agility in their integration landscape

The key question is not which platform is 'better', it is which platform is the right fit for your organisation's ecosystem, skill base, and strategic direction. For businesses running SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Ariba, or other SAP cloud solutions, the answer increasingly points to SAP Integration Suite.

 

2. Head-to-Head: Feature Comparison

The table below summarises verified, up-to-date comparisons across key integration criteria. 

 

Criteria

MuleSoft Anypoint Platform

SAP Integration Suite

Development effort

Higher complexity - Anypoint Studio (Eclipse-based IDE), Java/DataWeave expertise required. Steeper onboarding for new teams.

Lower effort - Browser-based graphical iFlow design. Pre-built SAP content packages accessible to developers and consultants. Low code, no code platform.

Message mapping

DataWeave used for mapping is proprietary and complex.

Graphical drag-and-drop mapping editor with built-in functions. Groovy/XSLT available for advanced cases. Easier to build and maintain.

Libraries

Need to import various Java libraries to develop integrations.

Most of the libraries are handled at the product level.

Event-driven architecture

No native built in event broker. External tools (RabbitMQ, Kafka, ActiveMQ) required, adding infrastructure cost and operational overhead.

SAP Event Mesh and Advanced Event Mesh is a fully managed native capability on BTP. Supports pub/sub, event streaming, multi-cloud distribution out of the box.

Transport & deployment

Changes to one flow require full application redeployment, less flexibility, risk involved and longer release cycles.

Artifact-level transport via Cloud Transport Management Service. Move individual iFlows independently across landscapes. Faster, safer releases.

SAP ecosystem connectivity

Third-party connectors or custom BAPI/RFC adapters needed for SAP systems. Additional build and maintenance effort.

Native connectors and thousands of pre-built packages on SAP Business Accelerator Hub. Zero custom work for core SAP integrations.

Custom adapter development

500+ connectors on Anypoint Exchange. SDK makes building custom connectors for non-SAP systems straightforward.

Strength in SAP-centric environment. Non-SAP custom adapters require deeper technical expertise.

Cost model

Premium vCore-based licensing. Costs can escalate unpredictably with integration volume growth.

BTP consumption-based model. Organisations with existing BTP entitlements get significant IS capacity included.

Monitoring & operations

Anypoint Monitoring provides dashboards and alerts. Comprehensive observability often requires custom configuration.

Simple Message monitoring, Alert Notification Service, Cloud ALM - all standard provided by out of the box by SAP. No additional tooling required.

Retry / error handling

Custom coding required (Until Successful scope or custom error flows). Risk of inconsistency across flows.

Built-in JMS-based retry with dead-letter queue handling. Persistent messaging, configurable without custom code.

Downtime handling

While MuleSoft CloudHub 2.0 offers zero-downtime via Rolling Updates and Blue-Green deployment, it requires specific replica configurations and vCore infrastructure overhead.

SAP Integration Suite provides seamless artifact deployment natively without additional infrastructure management.

Deployment Speed

MuleSoft deployments typically range from 3–10 minutes due to runtime initialization.

SAP Integration Suite typically completes in 10–45 seconds by updating the artifact in memory.

Salesforce Pub/Sub API(gRPC over HTTP/2)

While MuleSoft supports gRPC Connector with underlying high-performance API technology it often requires granular configuration, custom code or flow design to implement the same pub/sub streaming logic.

SAP Integration Suite provides a prebuilt Pub/Sub adapter that abstracts gRPC complexity, enabling configuration-driven consumption of Salesforce events without requiring protocol-level implementation.

 

MuleSoft offers flexibility at the cost of increased development complexity.

SAP Integration Suite reduces implementation effort and accelerates time-to-value

 

3. SAP Integration Suite — Key Strategic Differentiators

For organisations running SAP at their core, the following capabilities represent genuinely differentiated value that MuleSoft cannot replicate without additional investment.

 

BTP consumption pricing

Pay for what you use. Existing BTP entitlements often include significant Integration Suite capacity.

Advanced Event Mesh

Built-in event broker on BTP. No external Kafka/RabbitMQ needed. Real-time event streaming natively.

Artifact-level transport

UI driven, native transport mechanism to move individual iFlows across Dev → QA → Prod. No full application redeployment required.

 

Built-in monitoring

Message monitoring, Alert Notification Service, and Cloud ALM standard out of the box.

Business Accelerator Hub

Thousands of pre-built integration packages from SAP and partners. Reduces build effort significantly.

 

4. When Should Organisations Consider SAP Integration Suite?

Migration decisions should never be taken lightly. The following signals suggest a closer evaluation of SAP Integration Suite is warranted:

  • SAP-first digital core: Your organisation has standardised on S/4HANA and SAP cloud solutions. The majority of integration flows touch SAP systems.
  • MuleSoft licensing costs: Integration volumes are scaling and MuleSoft vCore costs are growing faster than the business value they deliver.
  • Legacy integration retirement: Moving off SAP PI/PO or MuleSoft - SAP Integration Suite is the natural destination for organisations consolidating on BTP.
  • Event-driven ambitions without broker overhead: If real-time event-driven patterns are on the roadmap, Event Mesh removes the need for external broker infrastructure.
  • Low integration cost: SAP Integration Suite is a low code platform requiring less development effort overall.

7. SAP Integration Suite & Salesforce — A Closer Look

Salesforce is the world's leading CRM platform. SAP is the world's leading ERP platform. Making them work together seamlessly is one of the most common, and most strategically important integration challenges enterprises face. This section gives an honest, balanced view of how SAP Integration Suite handles Salesforce integration.

An important nuance: MuleSoft is owned by Salesforce, giving it a structural first-party advantage in the Salesforce ecosystem - early API access, deep product alignment, and a large connector community. SAP Integration Suite wins on a different dimension listed above and primarily on SAP side of integration, where it has unmatched native depth.

SAP Integration Suite "Salesforce + SAP S4/HANA integration"

use cases

Use case

What it does

SAP IS advantage

Quote-to-cash

Salesforce opportunity or quote triggers SAP S4/HANA sales order creation automatically

Pre-built iFlow on SAP API Business Accelerator Hub. Native S/4HANA OData APIs used directly - no custom mapping layer needed

Customer master sync

Bidirectional sync of customer/account records between Salesforce and SAP

Handles complex SAP customer master data model models. Pre-built content manages mapping and prevents duplicate creation.

Order status updates

SAP fulfilment and delivery status pushed back to Salesforce in near-real-time

Event-driven via SAP Advanced Event Mesh. Sales reps see live order status without leaving CRM

Invoice and payment sync

SAP-generated invoices and payment status reflected in Salesforce.

Directly surfaces SAP FI/CO(Financial) data to Salesforce, ensuring accurate revenue tracking.

Real-time credit checks

Sales reps query SAP credit exposure directly from within Salesforce before quoting

On-demand SAP data pull via OData API, orchestrated by IS iFlow. Result returned to Salesforce in seconds

Event-driven order events

Salesforce Pub/Sub events consumed by SAP IS to trigger downstream SAP processes

Native Salesforce Pub/Sub adapter - real-time, no polling, no external broker required

Conclusion

For SAP-centric enterprises, SAP Integration Suite is not simply 'good enough' - it is increasingly the superior strategic choice. Key reasons (including for Salesforce integration):

•  Lower total cost of ownership when BTP entitlements are already in place.

•  Native connectivity eliminates custom adapter work for SAP applications.

•  Built-in Event Mesh removes the need for external broker infrastructure.

•  Artifact - level transport enables safer, faster release cycles.

•  Pre-packaged integration content from the Business Accelerator Hub accelerates delivery.

•  Better developer productivity due to out of box functionality by the platform.

•  Browser-based tooling integrated within the SAP ecosystem, enabling centralized development and management.

“This is an independent analysis based on project-based experience and publicly available information”

entry.title: Simplifying API Authentication with External OAuth in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Managing authentication across multiple identity providers can get complex—especially when partners bring their own identity systems.
With External OAuth (OIDC) in SAP Integration Suite, you can now validate and propagate tokens from providers like Microsoft Entra, Google, or Okta—enabling secure, seamless, and standardized authentication across systems.

This means:
· Trust external identity providers
· Reduce dependency on a single auth mechanism (like XSUAA)
· Enable smooth partner onboarding
· Simplify cross-platform API security

Learn more:

  • Blog post: Secure APIs: External OIDC-Based Authentication fo... - SAP Community
  • Help guide: https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/authentication-fa6eec4f9ffc45aa89…

entry.title: New Partner Content on SAP Business Accelerator Hub (Q1 ’26)
entry.content:
In continuation of our previous blog, we are excited to share the latest updates and continue delivering valuable insights to our community.

In this edition of our blog series, we highlight the newest content packages (particularly APIs and Integrations) released on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub by our partners in Q1 2026, along with high-level details.

For complete details on each partner's content, see the partners listing on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub Home page.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Avalara

Integration packages published:

SAP S/4HANA Integration with Avalara Avatax Sales and Use Tax

Sales and use tax rates are varied and complex across counties, states, and countries. And because the burden of remaining compliant is on the business, there’s a big financial risk to getting it wrong. Even when manual tax processes are manageable, keeping up with the changes can be impossible — there’s always the possibility of legislative regulations, new tax rules, and shifts in product taxability. Rather than spending time ensuring your rates, product taxability, and collection requirements haven’t changed, let our cloud-based SaaS system evaluate each transaction based on the latest laws and rules, without additional effort from your IT or finance teams.

SAP S/4HANA Integration with Avalara Solution Suite

Processes such as routing, authentication and logging that are reused across different integrations have been brought together in a single package.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Incture Technologies Private Limited

Integration packages published:

Microsoft Power BI Integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

The integration of Microsoft Power BI with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition provides a robust and scalable architecture for advanced analytics and business intelligence. This setup leverages SAP Cloud Integration as a secure and flexible middleware to extract, transform, and deliver SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition data to Microsoft Power BI, enabling real-time and insightful data visualization.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition with Shopify

Integration between SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition with Shopify connects Shopify’s e-commerce storefront with SAP’s ERP core. Shopify manages online sales and customer experience, while SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition handles order processing, inventory, pricing, fulfillment, and financials. Data is synchronized in near real time using SAP Cloud Integration.

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll To Banamex ACH

The integration between SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP) and Banamex ACH enables secure and automated transmission of payroll data from SAP’s cloud-based payroll system to the bank for salary disbursement. The solution ensures accurate, compliant, and timely processing of employee payments.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Figaf ApS

Integration package published:

Figaf Integration with ServiceNow

The "Figaf Integration with ServiceNow" package provides a template for integrating Figaf and SNow. It includes highly configurable iFlows processing Figaf webhooks for ticket/transport events to SNow actions, update Figaf based on SNow changes, and a utility for searching tasks.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

QuantityWare GmbH

Integration package published:

QuantityWare Integration with Third Party

The integration between QuantityWare Bulk Calculations Solution (BCS) and non-SAP environments through SAP BTP, provides customers the ability to access BCS from any application or external interface. This expands QuantityWare BCS usage beyond the SAP environment, allowing greater flexibility and integration potential.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPS Commerce Inc

Integration package published:

SPS Commerce Supplier Integration with SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud 

The SPS Commerce Fulfillment Connector for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud enables businesses to effortlessly automate and optimize electronic data interchange (EDI) with trading partners, streamlining order-to-cash processes and enhancing supply chain visibility. This cloud-based solution offers easy configuration, scalable performance, and a single connection to thousands of retailers, vendors, and logistics providers, reducing manual work and errors while supporting business growth. Integrated directly with SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud and the SAP Business Technology Platform through SAP Integration Suite, it provides real-time monitoring, compliance with trading partner requirements, and 24/7 expert support. By leveraging this connector, companies can consolidate orders across channels, achieve seamless data exchange, and maintain a clean core SAP environment.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

PriceFx AG

Integration packages published:

SAP S/4HANA Pricing Procedure Mapping with Pricefx

This integration package contains sample mappings required to replicate pricing procedures from SAP S/4HANA to Pricefx.

SAP S/4HANA Pricing Procedure Integration with Pricefx

This integration package contains integration flows to replicate pricing procedures from SAP S/4HANA to Pricefx. They can be used as templates to speed up your integration between SAP S/4HANA and Pricefx. You could modify message mappings and value mappings according to your needs.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SirionLabs Pte Ltd

Integration packages published:

Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management Integration with SAP Ariba Supplier Management

The Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) Integration with SAP Ariba Supplier Management enables seamless synchronization of supplier between SAP Ariba and Sirion CLM, ensuring a unified and accurate supplier lifecycle across systems.

Supplier requests approved in SAP Ariba triggers the creation of counterparty/supplier in Sirion CLM along with workflow movement ensuring complete synchronization of supplier's information.

Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management Integration with SAP Ariba Contract Workspace

The Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) Contract Workspace Adapter for SAP Ariba seamlessly integrates the contracting flow between the two systems. Contract Requests (CDRs) signed in Sirion CLM are automatically transitioned into Contract Workspaces (CWs) in SAP Ariba along with the associated contract documents, enabling synchronization between the two systems throughout the contracting flow.​

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

RealCore Group GmbH

Integration packages published:

SAP LeanIX Integration with SAP API Management

This integration package enables data synchronization between SAP API Management and SAP LeanIX factsheets by extracting relevant API proxies and provider information and transferring them to SAP LeanIX applications.

SAP Cloud ALM and Jira Task Synchronization

This integration package enables comprehensive and bidirectional synchronization of tasks between SAP Cloud ALM and Atlassian Jira, ensuring transparency and alignment across both platforms. It facilitates seamless collaboration between project and development teams by synchronizing all relevant task data in real time.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

KaTe GmbH

Integration package published:

KaTe Confluent Parallel Consumer Adapter

KaTe confluent parallel consumer adapter allows you to scale on record key level or even process records without order if required.

The adapter implements confluent parallel consumer lib to scale on different configurable metrics as single or batch processing option for high volume processing.

The adapter inherits all features of the KaTe Kafka adapter like support for Avro and Protobuf schemas, schema registries as well as sophisticated error handling and monitoring capabilities built into the SAP integrations suite environment.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Thank you very much for your attention, and we look forward to sharing more exciting content in the future.

If you are interested in Partnering with SAP Business Accelerator Hub, please refer to this section for more details.

entry.title: Positioning SAP Integration Suite enterprise-wide
entry.content:
Integration conversations are changing.  Fast.

Your customers are dealing with fragmented landscapes, rising costs, and growing pressure to deliver results across hybrid environments.  The question is no longer “how do we connect systems?”  It’s “how do we simplify and scale without losing control?”

In our upcoming webinar, Positioning SAP Integration Suite enterprise-wide, we’ll walk through what’s happening in the integration market today.  Where competitors shine.  Where gaps exist.  And where you, as a partner, can step in with clarity and confidence.

We’ll also break down how to position SAP Integration Suite as the backbone for enterprise-wide integration.  Think of fewer moving parts, faster innovation cycles, and measurable outcomes your customers can see.

Plus, you’ll get practical guidance.  How to spot the right entry points.  How to shape strong value narratives.  How to build and grow your integration practice over time.

If you’re already familiar with SAP Integration Suite, this session will help you connect the dots and turn strategy into action.

Register now.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/mar/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00013512.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #IntegrationStrategy #DigitalTransformation #EnterpriseArchitecture

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite Licensing: A Practical Guide for Businesses and Architects
entry.content:
Introduction

Within the SAP ecosystem, developers benefit from extensive documentation and technical tutorials. However, business leaders, enterprise architects, procurement teams, and startups often struggle to find clear guidance on licensing, pricing, and commercial models—particularly for SAP Integration Suite.

This blog bridges that gap by offering a practical, business-focused overview of SAP Integration Suite licensing, along with real-world case studies that illustrate how organizations can select the most suitable commercial model.

Why Licensing Information Is Hard to Find

Unlike technical documentation, commercial details are not widely publicized. Key reasons include:

1. Tailored Pricing Models

SAP solutions are customised based on:

  • Organization size and industry
  • Integration volumes and complexity
  • Geographic presence
  • Compliance requirements
  • Existing SAP investments

2. Sales-Led Engagement Model

Licensing information is typically provided through:

  • SAP account executives
  • Authorized SAP partners
  • Commercial proposals and Statements of Work

3. Consumption-Based Cloud Economics

SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) follows a consumption-based model where costs depend on usage metrics such as:

  • Integration runtime capacity
  • Messages processed
  • API calls and events
  • Compute and memory utilization
  • Additional capabilities like B2B Integration and API Management

4. Rapid Evolution of SAP Offerings

SAP frequently enhances its cloud offerings, making static pricing references less reliable over time.

Overview of SAP Integration Suite Commercial Models

1. Free Tier

Best For: Developers, students, and innovation teams

Purpose: Learning, experimentation, and prototyping

Benefits:

  • No cost
  • Ideal for proofs of concept
  • Hands-on experience

Limitations:

  • Restricted resources
  • Not intended for production use

2. Pay-As-You-Go (PAYG)

Best For: Startups, SMEs, and organizations with unpredictable workloads

Benefits:

  • No upfront commitment
  • Pay only for what you consume
  • Rapid scalability and flexibility

Considerations:

  • Requires cost governance
  • Expenses increase with scale

3. Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement (CPEA)

Best For: Large enterprises adopting multiple SAP BTP services

Benefits:

  • Prepaid cloud credits
  • Flexibility across SAP BTP services
  • Volume-based discounts
  • Centralized governance

Considerations:

  • Requires upfront financial commitment
  • Needs consumption planning

4. Subscription-Based Licensing

Best For: Organizations with stable and predictable workloads

Benefits:

  • Predictable costs
  • Simplified budgeting
  • Guaranteed capacity

Considerations:

  • Less flexibility compared to consumption models
  • Potential risk of underutilization

5. RISE with SAP

Best For: Organizations adopting SAP S/4HANA Cloud as part of digital transformation

Benefits:

  • Bundled services under a single contract
  • Simplified vendor management
  • Accelerated cloud adoption

Considerations:

  • Less granular pricing visibility
  • Best suited for SAP-centric landscapes

Decision Matrix: Choosing the Right Model

Business Scenario

Recommended Model

Learning and experimentation

Free Tier

Startups building their first integrations

Pay-As-You-Go

SMEs with growing integration needs

PAYG transitioning to CPEA

Enterprises adopting multiple BTP services

CPEA

Predictable, high-volume workloads

Subscription-Based Licensing

S/4HANA Cloud transformation initiatives

RISE with SAP

System integrators and consulting firms

PAYG for projects; CPEA for internal use

Key Cost Drivers for SAP Integration Suite

Cost Driver

Business Impact

Number of integrations

Influences runtime capacity

Message volume

Major contributor to consumption costs

API calls

Impacts API Management usage

B2B/EDI capabilities

Adds specialized costs

Number of environments (Dev, QA, Prod)

Increases consumption

Data transformation complexity

Affects processing requirements

High availability and scalability

Raises infrastructure costs

Security and compliance requirements

May increase operational overhead

Real-World Case Studies

Case Study 1: Startup Integrating SaaS Applications

Industry: HR Technology Startup

Challenge: Integrate a cloud-based HR platform with SAP S/4HANA for clients.

Solution: Pay-As-You-Go model.

Implementation:

  • Connected Salesforce, SAP S/4HANA, and SuccessFactors.
  • Leveraged API Management and Cloud Integration.
  • Scaled gradually based on customer demand.

Outcome:

  • Zero upfront investment.
  • Faster time-to-market.
  • Controlled operational expenses.

Why PAYG Was Ideal:

  • Unpredictable workloads.
  • Need for agility and cost efficiency.

Case Study 2: Manufacturing Enterprise Modernizing Legacy Systems

Industry: Global Manufacturing

Challenge: Replace point-to-point integrations across SAP ECC, Ariba, and third-party logistics providers.

Solution: Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement (CPEA).

Implementation:

  • Standardized enterprise integrations using SAP Integration Suite.
  • Enabled EDI and API-based partner connectivity.
  • Utilized BTP credits across multiple services.

Outcome:

  • Reduced integration complexity.
  • Improved supply chain visibility.
  • Optimized costs through prepaid credits.

Why CPEA Was Ideal:

  • Large-scale, enterprise-wide adoption.
  • Need for flexibility across multiple BTP services.

Case Study 3: Retail Chain Managing Seasonal Demand

Industry: Retail and E-Commerce

Challenge: Handle fluctuating integration volumes during festive and seasonal peaks.

Solution: Pay-As-You-Go model.

Implementation:

  • Integrated SAP Commerce Cloud with logistics and payment gateways.
  • Scaled integrations during peak sales periods.

Outcome:

  • Cost optimization during off-peak seasons.
  • Elastic scalability during high demand.

Why PAYG Was Ideal:

  • Variable workloads and seasonal spikes.

Case Study 4: Banking Institution Requiring Predictable Costs

Industry: Banking and Financial Services

Challenge: Ensure secure, compliant, and stable integrations across core banking systems.

Solution: Subscription-Based Licensing.

Implementation:

  • Integrated SAP and non-SAP systems with strict governance.
  • Ensured regulatory compliance and high availability.

Outcome:

  • Predictable budgeting and financial planning.
  • Stable, mission-critical operations.

Why Subscription Was Ideal:

  • Consistent transaction volumes.
  • Strong compliance requirements.

Case Study 5: Enterprise Migrating to SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Industry: Professional Services

Challenge: Transform legacy systems while migrating to SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

Solution: RISE with SAP.

Implementation:

  • Leveraged pre-packaged integrations and clean-core principles.
  • Enabled seamless connectivity across cloud applications.

Outcome:

  • Simplified vendor and contract management.
  • Accelerated digital transformation.

Why RISE with SAP Was Ideal:

  • Unified approach to ERP modernization and integration.

A Typical Adoption Journey

Most organizations follow this path:

Free Tier → Pay-As-You-Go → CPEA or Subscription → RISE with SAP

This approach enables businesses to:

  • Validate use cases
  • Optimize investments
  • Scale efficiently
  • Reduce financial risks

Essential Questions to Ask SAP or Partners

Before selecting a commercial model, consider asking:

  1. What metrics determine Integration Suite costs?
  2. Which capabilities are included in the license?
  3. How are non-production environments billed?
  4. Are there volume discounts available?
  5. What governance tools exist for cost monitoring?
  6. Can the commercial model be converted later?
  7. What is the estimated total cost of ownership over three years?
  8. How does pricing scale as the organization grows?

Recommended Resources

  • SAP Discovery Center 
  • SAP BTP Pricing Calculator
  • SAP Help Portal
  • SAP Community Blogs and Q&A
  • SAP Learning Hub and openSAP
  • Authorized SAP Partners and Account Executives

Key Takeaways

  • Pay-As-You-Go is a licensing model—not a license-free option.
  • CPEA provides flexibility and cost optimization for enterprises.
  • Subscription models offer predictable costs for stable workloads.
  • RISE with SAP simplifies ERP-led transformations.
  • Architects must align technical architecture with commercial strategy.
  • Understanding licensing early prevents costly redesigns later.

Conclusion

While developers benefit from extensive technical guidance, businesses and architects often lack clarity around licensing and pricing. By understanding SAP Integration Suite’s commercial models, organizations can make informed decisions that align technology investments with business goals.

Selecting the right licensing model ensures scalability, cost efficiency, and long-term success in the cloud integration journey.

Call to Action

Have you implemented SAP Integration Suite using PAYG, CPEA, Subscription, or RISE with SAP? Share your experiences and insights in the comments to help the SAP Community learn and grow together.

Thank you for reading!

Connect in LinkedIn : Naveen Kumar 

Disclaimer:
This article reflects personal insights and is intended for educational and informational purposes only. It does not represent the official position of SAP. Pricing, licensing, and commercial models are subject to change and may vary by region and customer agreement. For the most accurate and up-to-date information, please consult SAP directly or an authorized SAP partner. SAP and other SAP products mentioned herein are trademarks or registered trademarks of SAP SE in Germany and other countries.

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition - The “Trust Layer” for Agentic AI
entry.content:
For the last year, the world has been captivated by AI’s ability to answer. The next era belongs to AI that can act.

Agentic AI promises something bigger than copilots and chat interfaces: a digital workforce that can sense what’s happening, reason over context, and initiate the next best action across finance, supply chain, HR, procurement, and customer operations. But here’s the reality most enterprises discover quickly: the leap from clever prototype to production-grade agent isn’t a model problem — it’s a trust problem.

Trust is earned in the connective tissue of the enterprise: identity, governance, reliable integration, observability, and controlled execution across SAP and non‑SAP systems. That’s exactly why we’re excited to announce SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition — designed to help organizations move from AI experimentation to agentic outcomes by providing an integration foundation where agents can operate safely, securely, and at scale.

From “AI that knows” to “AI that does”: why integration becomes strategic

Agentic AI doesn’t live in a single application. It lives between applications — where approvals happen, where exceptions are resolved, where events trigger downstream actions, where APIs enforce guardrails, and where process continuity must be protected.

This is the moment when integration becomes more than plumbing. It becomes the execution fabric for AI.

SAP’s vision for Integration Suite is explicit: unify your enterprise with secure, AI‑ready integration and the governance required to scale agentic AI across the business — not as an add-on, but as an enterprise capability.

What’s new in enhanced edition — and why it’s built for agentic reality

SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition provides access to newly released AI capabilities that focus on two things agentic AI needs most:

1) Speed to value: AI that accelerates how integrations are built

Enhanced edition includes AI features aimed at boosting developer productivity, including:

  • Text‑to‑Flow generation — build integration flows using a natural language interface.
  • Script Optimization — analyze, refactor, and optimize Groovy or JavaScript scripts inside iFlows.

In an agentic world, integration teams must iterate faster — because agents will continuously evolve how they use tools, call APIs, and respond to events. These capabilities are about compressing the distance between an idea and a governed, deployable integration asset.

2) Operational trust: AI that helps integrations run with confidence

Agents introduce new scale and new patterns — and that means you need sharper operational insight. Enhanced edition includes AI-driven operations efficiency features such as:

  • API Anomaly Detection — AI-driven observability that detects unusual behavior and flags issues early.
  • API Traffic Prediction — identify trends and predict upcoming API call volumes to support smarter planning and load management.

This is what makes the positioning “agentic enabler” real: it’s not just that AI helps you build integrations — it helps you run the execution fabric with the resilience and foresight required for autonomous action.

More than AI features: the full suite that agents can safely act through

Enhanced edition also includes the broader platform capabilities needed to operationalize agentic scenarios end-to-end, including:

  • The full suite of integration capabilities (application integration, B2B integration, API management, edge integration) and broad connectivity.
  • Integration Suite, advanced event mesh for event-driven architecture use cases.
  • SAP Document AI, plus Alert Notification Service and Transport Management Service to support intelligent automation patterns and governed lifecycle management.

In other words: enhanced edition is designed to be a practical on‑ramp to a future where AI agents don’t just inform decisions — they execute decisions across the enterprise, under control.

How customers can move to enhanced edition (designed to be seamless)

Many customers already run SAP Integration Suite today — and moving to enhanced edition is intended to be an in-place plan upgrade rather than a disruptive reimplementation.

We provide documented upgrade guidance for updating an existing tenant to a higher service plan (with the note that it’s a commercial metadata update and is irreversible once applied). See official documentation on help.sap.com.
Additionally, our community guidance on tenant upgrades has been updated to include upgrade paths to the newly released enhanced_edition. See SAP Note 2903776.

For customers modernizing from older integration platforms, SAP also provides structured guidance and tools to support transition journeys to SAP Integration Suite. See migration factory page on sap.com.

Commercial options: choose the model that matches your AI journey

Agentic adoption is rarely linear — which is why commercial flexibility matters.

SAP BTP supports two commercial models:

  • Subscription-based: fixed cost for subscribed services.
  • Consumption-based: flexibility to use eligible services as business needs evolve, available via models such as SAP BTP Enterprise Agreement / Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement and Pay‑As‑You‑Go for SAP BTP.

And if your organization wants a low-friction entry point, choose Integration Suite free tier and a path to upgrade to a paid service plan.

You can find out more details on the different commercial models on SAP Discovery Centre.

A note on AI feature availability and rollout

The availability of AI features depend on the availability of SAP AI Core and the specific LLM model and corresponding version on that data centre. If you notice that AI features are not available in the DC of your choice, please get in touch with us via the comments section regarding your requirement.

The bigger picture: building an enterprise where AI can safely act

The most important shift isn’t “AI inside an app.” It’s AI as a participant in end-to-end business execution.

SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition is built for that shift — providing the integration fabric where autonomous agents can access governed tools, respond to events, operate across hybrid landscapes, and execute actions with observability and control.

If your organization is serious about moving from AI inspiration to agentic transformation, the most strategic question isn’t “Which model should we use?” It is: Do we have an integration foundation strong enough for AI to act — at enterprise scale?

Frequently Asked Questions on Enhanced Edition

What are the commercial transition options to Enhanced Edition?

If you are a consumption-based (BTPEA/CPEA) Integration Suite customer, you already have access to Enhanced Edition. Just upgrade your current tenants to Enhanced Edition via our seamless upgrade process. If you are a subscription customer, please get in touch with your customer success partner or account executive to understand the best path to obtain Enhanced Edition entitlements.

If you are a customer using one of our older standalone service licenses, please reach out to us via your customer success partner or account executive as well for more details on how to obtain Enhanced Edition entitlements.

What is the technical upgrade/downgrade options to/from Enhanced Edition?

Technically, we support a seamless upgrade from any of the current Integration Suite plan to the Enhanced Edition. More details here: upgrade process & upgrade paths

We do not support downgrading Enhanced Edition tenants to any of the other Integration Suite editions.

Can we have a mix of Standard/Premium tenant with Enhanced Edition in the same landscape?

We do not recommend this since it is critical to have feature parity across the different landscape tiers.

I don’t have AI units; can I still use AI features in Enhanced Edition?

Yes. Currently, we are offering AI features as a promotion. Soon, we will start charging the AI features via AI units. Please stay tuned to this space as more details will follow in the next weeks on this topic.

What happens if I reach the fair usage limit for AI features in Enhanced Edition?

You will have to wait for the next calendar month to begin when your usage quota gets reset. This is a limitation only till we launch metering AI feature usage via AI units, after which you can buy more quota and use AI features based on your requirement.

Is MCP already available on Enhanced Edition if I am a beta customer?

Not yet; it will be launched soon. Please refer to our roadmap for the updated timelines.

How can I purchase additional quota for the included services like Document AI, TMS, ANS, etc. in Enhanced Edition?

If you are on a consumption-based (BTPEA/CPEA) license or a hybrid (subscription+consumption) license, you already have access to the services via your credits and can use them, once you it the limits in Enhanced Edition.

If you are a subscription-only customer, please get in touch with your customer success manager or account executive to buy more quota.

Can I upgrade the AEM broker to a larger size? What happens to the existing entitlement if I upgrade?

Yes, the broker can be upgraded to a bigger size. You will either be charged on your consumption license or will have to procure a subscription license for the bigger broker of your choice before the upgrade process. 

The entitlement will continue to remain, and you can provision another standard-100 broker if you wish.

Is there a dedicated SKU for a non-production tenant of Enhanced Edition?

No. We only have one SKU for Enhanced Edition and it can be used in both production and non-production modes.

Is there a dedicated TDD SKU for Enhanced Edition?

This is currently work in progress and will be launched in the near future. Please stay tuned to this space for more information.

entry.title: Q1/2026 Product Highlights - SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Welcome to another quarterly recap of the latest released features in SAP Integration Suite! The new capabilities in Q1/2026 focus on better operations and smoother eventing for integration practitioners who build, run, and troubleshoot integrations every day.  Enjoy the highlights and check the linked docs and demos for how-to details and the road map for the full list.

Enhanced Edition is here

SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition helps move AI and agentic AI from pilots to production by providing governed, real-time connectivity, low-code developer tools, and AI-driven operations (Text-to-Flow, Script Optimization, API Anomaly Detection, API Traffic Prediction, plus connectors, event mesh, Document AI, alerting, and transport management). It reduces integration debt, enables secure agent actions across cloud and on-prem systems, and creates reusable building blocks for faster, repeatable AI value.

Accelerate your AI initiatives and make them enterprise-ready, get more details in the SAP Discovery Center.

Event-driven Integration

Discovery and publication of advanced event mesh events in the Developer Hub

Content administrators can discover and publish Advanced Event Mesh events directly in the Developer Hub, enabling events to be exposed as event products and making it easier for application developers to search and find relevant events. To use it, you create a destination to connect SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh to the Developer Hub; once connected, the business system appears in the Developer Hub for discovery and publication.

For more detailed instructions, check the documentation: Discover and Publish Events from Advanced Event Mesh in Developer Hub.

 

Migration support from SAP Event Mesh to SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh

This update provides technical and process-oriented support to help customers migrate from SAP Event Mesh (default plan) to SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, enabling organizations to move their event-driven architectures to a more powerful, feature-rich platform. The guide targets customers already using the SAP Event Mesh default plan and outlines steps to expand their event-based architecture. Read the guide: Migration From SAP Event Mesh to SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh.

Process Integration

Valkey support for Edge Integration Cell

The Edge Integration Cell capability in SAP Integration Suite now supports Valkey as an alternative datastore option alongside Redis and HANA DB. This option is available as selectable installation supporting versions 8.0-8.2 and allows administrators to choose Valkey for deployments helping reduce total cost of ownership.

For the steps and prerequesites, see: Deploy the Edge Integration Cell Solution

Monitor Message Processing on SAP Focused Run and SAP Cloud ALM

Users can monitor message processing from Edge Integration Cell using a push-based approach to send logs to SAP Focused Run (FRUN) and SAP Cloud ALM (CALM). This enables centralized monitoring of multiple Edge Integration Cell runtimes within a single application, lets you configure alerts based on message processing log status, and supports monitoring of Edge Integration Cell message processing logs in SAP Focused Run even during offline scenarios.

See Monitor Message Processing on SAP Focused Run and SAP Cloud ALM for more information.

Message queue management: extension and filtering of scenario details in the message queue monitor

The message queue monitor now displays scenario-specific attributes, such as Sender, Receiver, Application Message Type, Application Message ID, and Correlation ID, which can be enabled in the Messages table settings and used as filter criteria.

This feature lets you narrow messages more efficiently and quickly identify problematic entries with immediate context, reducing mean time to resolution by allowing direct filtering to relevant messages, enabling more precise troubleshooting and root-cause analysis, cutting operational noise by surfacing only the messages that matter, improving auditing and compliance with visible searchable metadata, and simplifying collaboration and handover between operations, support, and development teams. Get started: Managing Messages Stored in Queues

Support for parameterized mappings for the adapter type

The import options from SAP Process Orchestration into SAP Integration Suite - Cloud Integration have been extended to support parameters in message mappings for the adapter category, enabling you to import PI/PO message mappings that include adapter-type parameters; migration tooling and standalone message mapping import now accept mapping objects with the Adapter Type parameter category of type Import, which preserves adapter-specific parameterization, reduces manual adjustments and rework during migration, improves fidelity of migrated mappings, and accelerates onboarding and troubleshooting by maintaining runtime behavior tied to adapter parameters. See documentation: Import a Message Mapping from Enterprise Services Repository (ESR)

Connectivity Updates

Customer-managed cloud connectors

The Edge Integration Cell runtime now supports using a customer-managed external SAP Cloud Connector instead of the built-in, SAP-managed connector, allowing organizations to deploy the connector in a network zone that best fits their infrastructure and security requirements. This feature adds deployment flexibility, improves security controls, enables additional traffic inspection capabilities, and helps meet infrastructure and compliance needs. See configuration steps: Configure an External Cloud Connector for Edge Integration Cell

Additionally, users can now add and initialize an Edge Node with an external Cloud Connector through Edge Lifecycle Management. See how: Initializing an Edge Node with External Cloud Connector

Latest adapters

The list of released adapter in Q1/2026 includes:

  • BambooHR adapter: Connect to BambooHR to access APIs related to employee, organizational, time off data etc.
  • Zendesk adapter: Offers a comprehensive set of Ticketing CRUD operations for various entities such as Tickets, Attachments, Requests, Tags, etc. The adapter is designed to receive and process data from an integration flow in SAP Integration Suite and facilitate communication with Zendesk application (external) using REST-based APIs.
  • Microsoft 365 Outlook adapter: Enables seamless integration between Microsoft Outlook and SAP Integration Suite by improving communication and automating workflows between the two platforms. Available as a Sender and Receiver adapter. 🎥 Watch the demo. 
  • OFTP2 adapter: Use the Odette File Transfer Protocol (OFTP) adapter to exchange EDI and business files with partners on your Edge Integration Cell.
  • NFS File adapter: Enables connectivity to NFS server facilitating read and write operations for files on your Edge Integration Cell.  🎥 Watch the demo.

Updated data center availability

The new data center availability options for SAP Integration Suite include: Australia (Sydney) on SAP infrastructure, Japan (Tokyo) and USA on an SAP-hosted infrastructure, and the United Kingdom through Microsoft Azure.

---

Learn more

Want a sneak peek into what else is in store for SAP Integration Suite in 2026? Watch this walkthrough:

 What are the key innovation you're looking forward to in 2026? Let me know in the comments!

entry.title: SAP Cloud Integration (CI) for SAP IBP HPA integration: A real-world implementation case study
entry.content:
In one of the previous blog post, SAP Cloud Integration was introduced as new integration option for SAP IBP. Subsequent blog posts covered technical topics for creating integration flows based on SAP delivered packages and flows for different integration scenarios. These blog posts in addition to highlighting why organizations should consider SAP CI as part of their IBP integration strategy, covered fundamental set-up and configuration steps for using it for integrating SAP IBP with S/4 Hana Cloud.

In this blog, we explore a real-world example of an organization using Cloud Integration to overcome complex integration challenges for their IBP HPA implementation. We share details of challenges, key architectural decisions, and lessons learned from this complex supply chain planning implementation. This blog highlights a proven integration approach that offers valuable learnings for organizations navigating similar challenges.

Integration challenges: Distributed landscape

The organization in focus like other large manufacturing organizations operates an extensive global supply chain. Their landscape includes:

  • Multiple ERP system distributed globally
  • Large product portfolio with complex supply chain spread across locations using different systems

The key challenge was to integrate data from multiple SAP and Non-SAP systems. The integration approach needs to be flexible enough to support multiple source system integration with SAP IBP.

Key decision: Cloud Integration or Real Time Integration

One of the most critical decisions in any IBP implementation is to choose the right integration tool for IBP.  From release 2508 onwards, RTI and SAP CI are the two integration tools available to integrate SAP IBP HPA with SAP S4 Hana private cloud.

 

The sample customer’s implementation team made an early decision to go with a standalone CI integration approach for IBP. Some of the drivers for this decision were:

  • Cloud Integration works well with all SAP and non-SAP ERP systems, compared to RTI which is restricted only to S/4HANA Private edition
  • Readily available Cloud Integration expertise within the organization
  • Operational planning is carried out in ERP system, ruling out need for Order based planning in IBP
  • Business followed monthly planning cycle, with delta data load planned daily

Additionally, the availability of prepackaged reusable assets for SAP Cloud Integration supports accelerated implementation and reduces time to value significantly. It provides comprehensive connectivity options for diverse landscape, and it being under active development from SAP keeps your integration architecture future ready. Its web-based centralized monitoring and error handling capabilities help in reducing the overall maintenance cost. Because of these inherent advantages of SAP CI, the implementation team decided to go with standalone SAP CI integration architecture.

As real-time data sync capability was not needed, RTI didn’t offer any additional business value in comparison to CI in this scenario. However, depending on your business needs, Operational planning (OBP) in IBP and real time sync capabilities could be essential, in that case CI can be used in conjunction with RTI. As per SAP guidelines, integration architecture can utilize both the integration technologies following way:

  • RTI used for integrating master data and order data from S/4 Hana Private edition
  • SAP Cloud Integration (CI) used for all other integration scenarios such as Key Figures or other non-standard objects.

Following SAP help portal page helps you find right integration tool for your business needs: Finding the Right Integration Tool | SAP Help Portal

Integration design philosophy: keep interfaces “Lean” and “Scalable”

An important contributing factor to the successful implementation of CI for SAP IBP was to keep interfaces simple. The customer's implementation team followed a clear guiding principle that Cloud Integration (CI) should be used only for transporting data from one system to another. It should not be used for deriving any business logic. Cloud Integration (CI) is best used for:

  • Data transportation
  • Data format transformation and technical validation
  • Security handling

Business rules, process logic, and calculations were handled in the source or target system. This approach of preprocessing data in source or target system, made sure CI flows are simple, maintainable and reusable.

Preprocessing of data in source system:

To keep the core and IBP Add-on clean on the S/4 Hana, the implementation team decided to use extended CDS views for data preprocessing in S/4 Hana. Cloud integration flows read data directly from CDS views, avoiding modification of extractors and therefore sticking to clean core best practices.

How it works: Multi layered CDS views were created for preprocessing data in S/4 Hana:

  1. Reuse layer CDS views: Built on IBP add-on staging tables and raw tables (for additional data), this layer provided filtered data. As these CDS view also included data from raw tables, any custom data fields not available in IBP add-on staging tables were captured in these CDS views
  2. Consumption layer CDS views: This is primarily used for renaming S/4 fields to IBP fields. Reuse layer CDS views served as input for these CDS views. CI flows read data directly from these CDS views.

Above approach along with Change Data Capture (CDC) capability for CDS extraction allows delta loads for master data and S/4 Hana execution transaction data.

Reusable integration flows in CI:

SAP CI provides prepackaged reusable integration flows, that can be used to integrate all master data and transaction data types with minimal configuration changes. For the sample customer, data integration is executed by running a single prepackaged reusable CI iflow. One reusable integration flow handles all integration scenarios – all master data and key figures, with the help of flexible parameters. These parameters define:

  • Source CDS view to read in S/4 Hana
  • Target Master data and Key figures in IBP
  • Transfer mode: delta or full load

By sticking to preprocessing within the source system using CDS views and integrating using reusable flow, solution is flexible to accommodate future requirements. Any future business requirements can be handled by extending CDS views in the source system, avoiding any changes in the integration layer.

Integration with non-SAP system:

To integrate non-SAP ERP systems, data from multiple ERP systems is first integrated into a Data warehousing system. In Data warehousing system similar CDS view dependent process is used to preprocess data and then a reusable CI flow is used to pull data into IBP. This unified approach means consistent data structure regardless of the source system type.

Back integration to S4 Hana system:

IBP add-on staging tables enable a fire and forget approach for integrating planning output data from IBP to S/4 Hana. CI delivers data to S/4 IBP add-on tables and S/4 process them independently.  

With this lean integration approach, figure below shows high level data flow between different systems:

 

 Key takeaways: Some of the key learnings from this successful implementation of CI integration are:

  • Evaluating business requirements to select the right integration tool for your implementation. Depending on specific scenarios, CI only integration strategy can be effective.
  • Keep integration lean and focus on data transport only. Push transformation and business logic in source and target systems
  • Availability of prepackaged integration flow helps in accelerated implementation, and results in scalable and low maintenance integration solution

entry.title: Cloud Integration with Translation Hub
entry.content:
Introduction:

In this blog post, I will demonstrate how to connect Cloud Integration with Translation Hub. As a use case, consider a scenario where a sender system submits an order request. If the order creation fails, the system expects the error message to be returned in both English and Japanese.

 

Translation Hub:

For an overview of the SAP Translation Hub service and its features, refer to discovery center.

 

Step 1:

Create an instance for a service named SAP Translation Hub (technical name: document-translation) and generate its corresponding service key.

From the service key, retrieve ‘documenttranslation.url’, ‘uaa.clientid’, ‘uaa.clientsecret’ and ‘uaa.url’, which will be later used in integration scenario.

Step 2:

Explore document translation api at Business Accelerator Hub.

For the model parameter, two values are supported: default and llm. The default model uses SAP-trained Neural Machine Translation (NMT). For a list of supported languages, refer to the SAP Translation Hub documentation: Supported Languages

The llm falls under the generative AI category and can be used when a target language is not supported by the SAP-trained NMT model. For example, to translate text into Bengali.

 

Cloud Integration:

Step 1:

Create a security material of type ‘OAuth2 Client Credentials’.

Step 2:

Design the iFlow as illustrated below.

The Content Modifier is used to set the message header and body.  As only the translated text is required, the ‘Accept’ header is set to ‘application/octet-stream’.

The request body is configured as shown below:

Configure the HTTP receiver adapter to connect with document translation api as shown below.

Step 3:

Test the iFlow endpoint using an API testing tool. In the request payload, ‘orderId’ is a mandatory field as defined in the XML Validator step; therefore, any payload that does not include ‘orderId’ is routed to an exception subprocess.

Please note that all the data are fictitious.

 

Conclusion:

This blog post demonstrates a prototype for integrating Translation Hub with Cloud Integration.
